---
title: "Digital Twins for Smart Cities: A Bibliometric Analysis of the Decision Support Gap"
subtitle: "ADM 804 — COPPEAD/UFRJ"
author: "Leonardo Mello Camilo da Silva"
date: today
format:
  pdf:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    number-sections: true
    fig-width: 10
    fig-height: 6
    documentclass: article
    geometry: margin=2.5cm
    fontsize: 11pt
    colorlinks: true
    keep-tex: false
execute:
  warning: false
  message: false
  echo: false
---

```{r}
#| label: setup
#| include: false

library(bibliometrix)
library(tidyverse)
library(igraph)
library(ggraph)
library(ggrepel)
library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)
library(here)
library(wordcloud2)
library(webshot2)

# Importar os .bib filtrados (3 categorias SCImago: Urban Studies + Geography/Planning/Dev + Decision Sciences)
df_scopus <- convert2df(
  file = here("data", "processed", "scopus_2_filt.bib"),
  dbsource = "scopus", format = "bibtex"
)
df_wos <- convert2df(
  file = here("data", "processed", "wos_2_filt.bib"),
  dbsource = "wos", format = "bibtex"
)
df <- mergeDbSources(df_scopus, df_wos, remove.duplicated = TRUE)

# Remover duplicatas residuais por título
df <- df %>% distinct(TI, .keep_all = TRUE)

# Extrair metadados de país
df <- metaTagExtraction(df, Field = "AU_CO", sep = ";")

# Análise geral do bibliometrix
resultados <- biblioAnalysis(df)

cat("Corpus carregado:", nrow(df), "artigos únicos\n")
```

# Visão Geral do Corpus

```{r}
#| label: estatisticas-gerais

stats_df <- data.frame(
  Métrica = c(
    "Total de artigos",
    "Período",
    "Periódicos únicos",
    "Autores únicos",
    "Países",
    "Média de citações por artigo",
    "Mediana de citações",
    "Total de citações"
  ),
  Valor = c(
    nrow(df),
    paste(min(df$PY, na.rm = TRUE), "–", max(df$PY, na.rm = TRUE)),
    n_distinct(df$SO),
    length(unique(unlist(strsplit(df$AU, ";")))),
    n_distinct(resultados$Countries),
    round(mean(df$TC, na.rm = TRUE), 1),
    median(df$TC, na.rm = TRUE),
    sum(df$TC, na.rm = TRUE)
  )
)

kable(stats_df, caption = "Table 1 — General statistics of the bibliometric corpus")
```

# Crescimento da Área

## Publicações por ano

```{r}
#| label: fig-evolucao-temporal
#| fig-cap: "Annual scientific production on digital twins and smart cities"

pub_ano <- df %>%
  count(PY, name = "Artigos") %>%
  filter(!is.na(PY)) %>%
  arrange(PY)

ggplot(pub_ano, aes(x = PY, y = Artigos)) +
  geom_line(color = "#2C3E50", linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(color = "#2C3E50", size = 3) +
  geom_text(aes(label = Artigos), vjust = -1, size = 3.5) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(min(pub_ano$PY), max(pub_ano$PY), 1)) +
  labs(x = "Year", y = "Number of Articles") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

ggsave(here("output", "figures", "fig_evolucao_temporal.png"),
       width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 300)
```

# Autores Mais Prolíficos

```{r}
#| label: tbl-top-autores

top_autores <- resultados$Authors %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  head(10)
names(top_autores) <- c("Author", "Articles")
top_autores$Articles <- as.numeric(top_autores$Articles)

kable(top_autores, caption = "Table 2 — Top 10 most prolific authors")
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-top-autores
#| fig-cap: "Top 10 most prolific authors"

ggplot(top_autores, aes(x = reorder(Author, Articles), y = Articles)) +
  geom_col(fill = "#8E44AD", width = 0.7) +
  geom_text(aes(label = Articles), hjust = -0.2, size = 3.5) +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "Number of Articles") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13)

ggsave(here("output", "figures", "fig_top_autores.png"),
       width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 300)
```

# Evolução Temporal dos Autores

Este gráfico mostra a produção dos autores mais prolíficos ao longo do tempo.
Cada ponto representa um artigo; o tamanho do ponto é proporcional ao número de citações recebidas.

```{r}
#| label: fig-author-prod-over-time
#| fig-cap: "Author production over time — top 10 authors (point size proportional to citations)"
#| fig-height: 8

# authorProdOverTime() do bibliometrix
apt <- authorProdOverTime(df, k = 10, graph = FALSE)

# Gráfico customizado de pontos + linha
p_apt <- ggplot(apt$dfAU, aes(x = year, y = Author, size = TCpY)) +
  geom_point(color = "#2C3E50", alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_line(aes(group = Author), color = "#2C3E50", linewidth = 0.4, alpha = 0.5) +
  scale_size_continuous(range = c(2, 8), name = "Citations/Year") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(min(apt$dfAU$year), max(apt$dfAU$year), 1)) +
  labs(x = "Year", y = NULL) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

print(p_apt)

ggsave(here("output", "figures", "fig_author_prod_over_time.png"),
       plot = p_apt, width = 10, height = 8, dpi = 300)
```

```{r}
#| label: tbl-author-prod-over-time

# Tabela resumo da produção dos autores ao longo do tempo
apt$dfPapersAU %>%
  head(15) %>%
  mutate(
    TI = ifelse(nchar(TI) > 50, paste0(substr(TI, 1, 47), "..."), TI),
    SO = ifelse(nchar(SO) > 30, paste0(substr(SO, 1, 27), "..."), SO)
  ) %>%
  kable(
    caption = "Table 3 — Author production over time (selected entries)",
    col.names = c("Author", "Year", "Title", "Journal", "DOI", "TC", "TC/Y"),
    booktabs = TRUE
  ) %>%
  kable_styling(font_size = 8, latex_options = c("hold_position", "scale_down")) %>%
  column_spec(1, width = "1.3cm") %>%
  column_spec(2, width = "1.2cm") %>%
  column_spec(3, width = "5cm") %>%
  column_spec(4, width = "3cm") %>%
  column_spec(5, width = "3cm") %>%
  column_spec(6, width = "1cm") %>%
  column_spec(7, width = "1.2cm")
```

# Centralidade dos Autores na Rede de Co-autoria

A centralidade mede a importância de cada autor na rede de colaboração. Autores com alta *betweenness centrality* são pontes entre grupos de pesquisa; autores com alto *degree* têm muitos co-autores diretos.

```{r}
#| label: tbl-centralidade-autores

# Construir rede de co-autoria
rede_autores <- biblioNetwork(df, analysis = "collaboration", network = "authors")

# Converter para igraph
g <- graph_from_adjacency_matrix(rede_autores, mode = "undirected", weighted = TRUE, diag = FALSE)

# Calcular métricas de centralidade
cent_df <- data.frame(
  Author = V(g)$name,
  Degree = degree(g),
  Betweenness = round(betweenness(g, normalized = TRUE), 4),
  Closeness = round(closeness(g, normalized = TRUE), 4),
  PageRank = round(page_rank(g)$vector, 4)
) %>%
  arrange(desc(Betweenness)) %>%
  head(15)

kable(cent_df,
      caption = "Table 4 — Top 15 authors by betweenness centrality in the co-authorship network")
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-centralidade-autores
#| fig-cap: "Top 15 authors by betweenness centrality"

ggplot(cent_df, aes(x = reorder(Author, Betweenness), y = Betweenness)) +
  geom_col(fill = "#E67E22", width = 0.7) +
  geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.3f", Betweenness)), hjust = -0.1, size = 3) +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "Betweenness Centrality (normalized)") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13)

ggsave(here("output", "figures", "fig_centralidade_autores.png"),
       width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 300)
```

# Top Periódicos

```{r}
#| label: tbl-top-journals

top_journals <- df %>%
  count(SO, name = "Articles", sort = TRUE) %>%
  head(10)

kable(top_journals, col.names = c("Journal", "Articles"),
      caption = "Table 5 — Top 10 most productive journals")
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-top-journals
#| fig-cap: "Top 10 most productive journals"

ggplot(top_journals, aes(x = reorder(SO, Articles), y = Articles)) +
  geom_col(fill = "#27AE60", width = 0.7) +
  geom_text(aes(label = Articles), hjust = -0.2, size = 3.5) +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "Number of Articles") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9))

ggsave(here("output", "figures", "fig_top_journals.png"),
       width = 12, height = 6, dpi = 300)
```

# Top Países Produtores

```{r}
#| label: tbl-top-paises

top_paises <- resultados$Countries %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  head(10)
names(top_paises) <- c("Country", "Articles")
top_paises$Articles <- as.numeric(top_paises$Articles)

kable(top_paises, caption = "Table 6 — Top 10 countries by scientific production")
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-top-paises
#| fig-cap: "Top 10 countries by scientific production"

ggplot(top_paises, aes(x = reorder(Country, Articles), y = Articles)) +
  geom_col(fill = "#3498DB", width = 0.7) +
  geom_text(aes(label = Articles), hjust = -0.2, size = 3.5) +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "Number of Articles") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13)

ggsave(here("output", "figures", "fig_top_paises.png"),
       width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 300)
```

# Top Artigos Mais Citados

```{r}
#| label: tbl-top-citados

top_citados <- df %>%
  select(AU, TI, SO, PY, TC) %>%
  arrange(desc(TC)) %>%
  head(10) %>%
  mutate(
    Author = sapply(strsplit(AU, ";"), function(x) {
      if (length(x) > 1) paste0(trimws(x[1]), " et al.")
      else trimws(x[1])
    }),
    Title = ifelse(nchar(TI) > 70, paste0(substr(TI, 1, 67), "..."), TI)
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    Journal = ifelse(nchar(SO) > 35, paste0(substr(SO, 1, 32), "..."), SO)
  ) %>%
  select(Author, Title, Journal, Year = PY, Citations = TC)

kable(top_citados, caption = "Table 7 — Top 10 most cited articles",
      booktabs = TRUE) %>%
  kable_styling(font_size = 8, latex_options = c("hold_position", "scale_down")) %>%
  column_spec(1, width = "2.5cm") %>%
  column_spec(2, width = "5.5cm") %>%
  column_spec(3, width = "3.5cm") %>%
  column_spec(4, width = "1.2cm") %>%
  column_spec(5, width = "1.5cm")
```

# Rede de Co-autoria

```{r}
#| label: fig-rede-coautoria
#| fig-cap: "Co-authorship network — clusters represent research groups"
#| fig-height: 8

set.seed(804)
net_autores <- networkPlot(
  rede_autores,
  n = 30,
  type = "fruchterman",
  Title = "Co-authorship Network",
  size.cex = TRUE,
  label.cex = TRUE,
  edgesize = 3,
  edges.min = 2,
  community.repulsion = 0.1
)
```

```{r}
#| label: save-rede-coautoria
#| include: false

png(here("output", "figures", "fig_rede_coautoria.png"),
    width = 10, height = 8, units = "in", res = 300)
set.seed(804)
networkPlot(rede_autores, n = 30, type = "fruchterman",
            Title = "Co-authorship Network",
            size.cex = TRUE, label.cex = TRUE,
            edgesize = 3, edges.min = 2, community.repulsion = 0.1)
dev.off()
```

# Rede de Colaboração entre Países

```{r}
#| label: fig-rede-paises
#| fig-cap: "International collaboration network between countries"
#| fig-height: 8

rede_paises <- biblioNetwork(df, analysis = "collaboration", network = "countries")

set.seed(804)
net_paises <- networkPlot(
  rede_paises,
  n = 20,
  type = "fruchterman",
  Title = "International Collaboration Network",
  size.cex = TRUE,
  label.cex = TRUE,
  edgesize = 5,
  edges.min = 2,
  community.repulsion = 0.1
)
```

```{r}
#| label: save-rede-paises
#| include: false

png(here("output", "figures", "fig_rede_paises.png"),
    width = 10, height = 8, units = "in", res = 300)
set.seed(804)
networkPlot(rede_paises, n = 20, type = "fruchterman",
            Title = "International Collaboration Network",
            size.cex = TRUE, label.cex = TRUE,
            edgesize = 5, edges.min = 2, community.repulsion = 0.1)
dev.off()
```

# Author Coupling

O acoplamento bibliográfico (bibliographic coupling) conecta autores que citam as mesmas referências, revelando frentes de pesquisa com base intelectual comum.

```{r}
#| label: fig-author-coupling
#| fig-cap: "Bibliographic coupling network between authors"
#| fig-height: 8

# Verificar se campo CR está disponível
cr_disponivel <- any(!is.na(df$CR) & df$CR != "" & df$CR != "none")

if (cr_disponivel) {
  rede_coupling <- tryCatch(
    biblioNetwork(df, analysis = "coupling", network = "authors"),
    error = function(e) { cat("Coupling error:", e$message, "\n"); NULL }
  )

  if (!is.null(rede_coupling)) {
    set.seed(804)
    net_coupling <- networkPlot(
      rede_coupling,
      n = 30,
      type = "fruchterman",
      Title = "Bibliographic Coupling — Authors",
      size.cex = TRUE,
      label.cex = TRUE,
      edgesize = 3,
      edges.min = 5,
      community.repulsion = 0.1
    )

    png(here("output", "figures", "fig_author_coupling.png"),
        width = 10, height = 8, units = "in", res = 300)
    set.seed(804)
    networkPlot(rede_coupling, n = 30, type = "fruchterman",
                Title = "Bibliographic Coupling — Authors",
                size.cex = TRUE, label.cex = TRUE,
                edgesize = 3, edges.min = 5, community.repulsion = 0.1)
    dev.off()
  } else {
    cat("Bibliographic coupling could not be computed.\n")
  }
} else {
  cat("Cited references (CR) not available. Bibliographic coupling requires re-export from Scopus/WoS with 'Include references'.\n")
}
```

# Contagem de Palavras-chave

```{r}
#| label: tbl-keywords

kw_autor <- df$DE %>%
  na.omit() %>%
  strsplit(";") %>%
  unlist() %>%
  trimws() %>%
  tolower() %>%
  table() %>%
  sort(decreasing = TRUE) %>%
  as.data.frame()
names(kw_autor) <- c("Keyword", "Freq")

kable(head(kw_autor, 20), caption = "Table 8 — Top 20 author keywords")
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-keywords
#| fig-cap: "Top 20 author keywords by frequency"

ggplot(head(kw_autor, 20), aes(x = reorder(Keyword, Freq), y = Freq)) +
  geom_col(fill = "#E74C3C", width = 0.7) +
  geom_text(aes(label = Freq), hjust = -0.2, size = 3.5) +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "Frequency") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13)

ggsave(here("output", "figures", "fig_top_keywords.png"),
       width = 10, height = 8, dpi = 300)
```

# Wordcloud de Palavras-chave

```{r}
#| label: fig-wordcloud
#| fig-cap: "Word cloud of author keywords"

# Preparar dados para wordcloud
wc_data <- head(kw_autor, 80)

# Gerar wordcloud como imagem estática via base R wordcloud
# (wordcloud2 gera HTML, difícil de capturar em PDF)
if (requireNamespace("wordcloud", quietly = TRUE)) {
  library(wordcloud)
  png(here("output", "figures", "fig_wordcloud.png"),
      width = 10, height = 8, units = "in", res = 300)
  par(mar = c(0, 0, 0, 0))
  wordcloud(
    words = wc_data$Keyword,
    freq = wc_data$Freq,
    min.freq = 1,
    max.words = 80,
    random.order = FALSE,
    rot.per = 0.15,
    colors = brewer.pal(8, "Dark2"),
    scale = c(3, 0.5)
  )
  dev.off()
  knitr::include_graphics(here("output", "figures", "fig_wordcloud.png"))
} else {
  # Fallback: usar wordcloud2 salvando como HTML + screenshot
  wc <- wordcloud2(wc_data, size = 0.6, color = "random-dark")
  print(wc)
}
```

# Rede de Co-ocorrência de Palavras-chave

```{r}
#| label: fig-rede-keywords
#| fig-cap: "Keyword co-occurrence network"
#| fig-height: 8

rede_kw <- biblioNetwork(df, analysis = "co-occurrences", network = "keywords")

set.seed(2024)
net_kw <- networkPlot(
  rede_kw,
  n = 40,
  type = "fruchterman",
  Title = "Keyword Co-occurrence Network",
  size.cex = TRUE,
  label.cex = TRUE,
  edgesize = 3,
  edges.min = 3,
  community.repulsion = 0.1
)
```

```{r}
#| label: save-rede-keywords
#| include: false

png(here("output", "figures", "fig_rede_keywords.png"),
    width = 10, height = 8, units = "in", res = 300)
set.seed(2024)
networkPlot(rede_kw, n = 40, type = "fruchterman",
            Title = "Keyword Co-occurrence Network",
            size.cex = TRUE, label.cex = TRUE,
            edgesize = 3, edges.min = 3, community.repulsion = 0.1)
dev.off()
```

# Mapa Temático

O mapa temático posiciona os clusters de palavras-chave em quatro quadrantes:

- **Motor themes** (alta centralidade + alta densidade): temas centrais e bem desenvolvidos
- **Niche themes** (baixa centralidade + alta densidade): temas periféricos mas coesos
- **Basic themes** (alta centralidade + baixa densidade): temas centrais mas pouco desenvolvidos
- **Emerging/declining themes** (baixa centralidade + baixa densidade): temas periféricos e pouco desenvolvidos

```{r}
#| label: fig-mapa-tematico
#| fig-cap: "Thematic map — strategic positioning of research themes"
#| fig-height: 10

mapa <- thematicMap(
  df,
  field = "DE",
  n = 250,
  minfreq = 3,
  stemming = FALSE,
  size = 0.5,
  repel = TRUE
)

print(mapa$map)

ggsave(here("output", "figures", "fig_mapa_tematico.png"),
       plot = mapa$map, width = 12, height = 10, dpi = 300)
```

```{r}
#| label: tbl-clusters-mapa

clusters_info <- mapa$clusters

clusters_display <- clusters_info %>%
  mutate(
    Quadrant = case_when(
      rcentrality >= 0.5 & rdensity >= 0.5 ~ "Motor",
      rcentrality < 0.5 & rdensity >= 0.5 ~ "Niche",
      rcentrality >= 0.5 & rdensity < 0.5 ~ "Basic",
      TRUE ~ "Emerging/Declining"
    )
  ) %>%
  select(Cluster = name, Centrality = rcentrality, Density = rdensity, Quadrant, Keywords = words)

kable(clusters_display, digits = 3,
      caption = "Table 9 — Thematic clusters from the strategic diagram")

write_csv(clusters_info, here("output", "tables", "clusters_mapa_tematico.csv"))
```

# Evolução Temática

```{r}
#| label: fig-evolucao-tematica
#| fig-cap: "Thematic evolution over time"

ano_min <- min(df$PY, na.rm = TRUE)
ano_max <- max(df$PY, na.rm = TRUE)
ano_corte <- floor((ano_min + ano_max) / 2)

evolucao <- tryCatch({
  thematicEvolution(df, field = "DE", years = c(ano_corte), n = 100, minFreq = 3)
}, error = function(e) {
  cat("Thematic evolution error:", e$message, "\n")
  tryCatch(
    thematicEvolution(df, field = "DE",
                      years = c(ano_min + 2, ano_max - 2),
                      n = 100, minFreq = 2),
    error = function(e2) { cat("Persistent error:", e2$message, "\n"); NULL }
  )
})

if (!is.null(evolucao)) {
  plotThematicEvolution(evolucao$Nodes, evolucao$Edges)
}
```

# Análise do Gap — Suporte à Decisão

```{r}
#| label: tbl-gap-analysis

# Verificar presença de termos de decisão/gestão nas keywords
termos_gap <- c(
  "decision support", "decision making", "decision-making",
  "public management", "public administration", "urban governance",
  "governance", "policy", "public policy", "urban management",
  "municipal", "city management"
)

gap_df <- data.frame(
  Term = termos_gap,
  Frequency = sapply(termos_gap, function(t) {
    f <- kw_autor$Freq[kw_autor$Keyword == t]
    if (length(f) == 0) 0 else f
  })
)

kable(gap_df, caption = "Table 10 — Frequency of decision-support and public management terms in author keywords")
```

```{r}
#| label: gap-clusters

# Verificar nos clusters do mapa temático
termos_decisao <- c("decision", "governance", "management", "policy",
                     "public", "municipal", "administration")

clusters_com_decisao <- clusters_info %>%
  filter(str_detect(tolower(words), paste(termos_decisao, collapse = "|")))

if (nrow(clusters_com_decisao) > 0) {
  cat("Clusters containing decision/management terms:\n\n")
  for (i in seq_len(nrow(clusters_com_decisao))) {
    centralidade <- clusters_com_decisao$rcentrality[i]
    densidade <- clusters_com_decisao$rdensity[i]
    quadrante <- case_when(
      centralidade >= 0.5 & densidade >= 0.5 ~ "MOTOR",
      centralidade < 0.5 & densidade >= 0.5 ~ "NICHE",
      centralidade >= 0.5 & densidade < 0.5 ~ "BASIC",
      TRUE ~ "EMERGING/DECLINING"
    )
    cat(sprintf("  '%s' → %s (centrality: %.3f, density: %.3f)\n",
                clusters_com_decisao$name[i], quadrante, centralidade, densidade))
  }
} else {
  cat("No thematic cluster contains decision-support or public management terms.\n")
  cat("This constitutes strong evidence of the identified research gap.\n")
}
```

# Exportar Tabelas

```{r}
#| label: exportar-tabelas
#| include: false

write_csv(pub_ano, here("output", "tables", "publicacoes_por_ano.csv"))
write_csv(top_journals, here("output", "tables", "top_journals.csv"))
write_csv(top_citados, here("output", "tables", "top_citados.csv"))
write_csv(stats_df, here("output", "tables", "estatisticas_gerais.csv"))
write_csv(cent_df, here("output", "tables", "centralidade_autores.csv"))
write_csv(gap_df, here("output", "tables", "gap_analysis.csv"))
```
